commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=de94cb1f71ab33832e4305022a38ba9862941917)
commands.review_summary:
The branch extends agent failover error classification and tests so code-only OpenAI, Google-family, and Anthropic structured errors map to failover reasons and no longer count as empty responses.

PR surface: Source +39, Tests +77. Total +116 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection, not from a local test run because this review is read-only. Current main’s code-only classifier lacks these provider-native mappings, and the runtime-kind classifier treats message-less/status-less structured code signals as `empty_response`.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer follow-up is needed to move the mapping to provider hooks and add real behavior proof; automation should not repair this external PR while proof is missing.

target.title: fix: classify provider code-only failover errors
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes code-only provider failover classification for structured assistant errors. When provider responses only expose an error `code` and no HTTP status/body message, several provider-native codes were falling through to `null`, so failover did not get the intended reason.

Covered code-only mappings:

- OpenAI `SERVER_ERROR` -> `server_error`
- OpenAI `INSUFFICIENT_QUOTA` -> `billing`
- Google-owned providers (`google`, `google-vertex`, `google-antigravity`) `UNAVAILABLE` -> `overloaded`
- Google-owned providers (`google`, `google-vertex`, `google-antigravity`) `DEADLINE_EXCEEDED` -> `timeout`
- Google-owned providers (`google`, `google-vertex`, `google-antigravity`) `INTERNAL` -> `server_error`
- Anthropic `RATE_LIMIT_ERROR` -> `rate_limit`
- Anthropic `API_ERROR` -> `timeout`

## Why This Change Was Made

`classifyFailoverReasonFromCode` already normalized code strings and handled common rate-limit/overload/auth/Node timeout codes, but it missed provider-native OpenAI, Google, and Anthropic code values. The fix extends that existing code-only classifier with exact provider-id guards so OpenAI-compatible or gateway codes are not misclassified. The adjacent runtime failure classifier also treats structured code-only signals as real provider failures instead of reporting them as empty responses.

## User Impact

Model failover now works correctly for structured provider errors that only carry these codes. Users should see fewer stuck primary-provider failures when the provider omits status/body details, without changing unrelated OpenAI-compatible provider behavior.

## Evidence

- Reproduced on latest `main`: the new code-only assistant error regression cases returned `null` before the fix.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.isbillingerrormessage.test.ts` passes, 160/160.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo` passes.
- `git diff --check` passes.
- `OPENCLAW_PLUGIN_SDK_MAX_PUBLIC_FUNCTION_EXPORTS=5183 node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check` fails as expected with `public callable exports 5184 > 5183`, proving this PR does not add a plugin SDK callable export.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` reported no accepted/actionable findings after the provider-aware guards and runtime-kind fix.
target.author: pick-cat
